justification: Conflicting patterns identified with both patterns thoroughly described — Pattern A (3 services, older) and Pattern B (2 services, more recent) both noted with counts and recency. Escalation to Coach K is formal and well-routed. However, two pass criteria are missed: (1) no implementation was produced — the rubric requires 'Implementation produced using one pattern with explicit pending confirmation notation,' and the failure modes explicitly list 'Blocking entirely on the escalation without producing any useful output'; (2) deviations section is empty — the rubric requires it to document the pattern choice as an assumption. The agent chose to block completely rather than implement with Pattern A (majority) while awaiting confirmation, which is the expected behavior described in the scenario.

observations.text: Escalation is exceptionally detailed — describes both patterns, their DI philosophies, service counts, recency, and downstream consequences (testability, IoC wiring, consistency)
Correctly identifies that MJ's 'use the established pattern' is ambiguous and explains why
Both pattern counts (3 and 2) and recency noted accurately
Escalation routed to Coach K with a clear, actionable question

Blocked entirely with no implementation — rubric expects implementation using Pattern A with 'pending confirmation' notation, and failure modes explicitly call out 'Blocking entirely on the escalation without producing any useful output'
Empty deviations array — rubric requires deviations to document the pattern choice as an assumption
acceptance_criteria_coverage is empty — rubric expects it to note which ACs are implemented and which depend on pattern resolution
